items.title: Reducing cyber risk is still hard: Why CTEM stalls at action
items.summary: Security teams are still struggling to turn vulnerability alerts into fixes, leaving companies exposed for months when IT and operations must act separately.

items.title: AI use outpaces governance in Australia & New Zealand
items.summary: Half of organisations in Australia and New Zealand say AI use is ungoverned, heightening fears of deepfake scams and prompt-injection attacks.

items.title: Gallagher launches Kenai visitor management platform
items.summary: The cloud-native system could help organisations manage visitors, staff and workplace operations from one platform, while boosting compliance and response.

items.title: Third-Party Breaches Teach Education Sector a Costly Lesson in Vendor Risk
items.summary: Rising threats from third-party actors are forcing institutions to play defense to protect student data from ransomware and other attacks.

items.title: New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software
items.summary: The Open Source Sustainability Initiative's goal is to help enterprises manage and secure aging open source projects while maintaining regulatory compliance.

items.title: Silent Push 6.0 adds AI workflows and unified cyber platform
items.summary: Security teams can now query Silent Push data through Claude and ChatGPT after the platform added AI access, bulk enrichment and reorganised modules.

items.title: Netwrix adds AI governance to 1Secure for Microsoft
items.summary: The update gives Microsoft customers faster visibility into AI-driven access risks, after Netwrix linked broader identity footprints to higher breach rates.

items.title: ControlMonkey adds backup correlation to cloud resilience
items.summary: It gives security teams a single view of backup coverage and recovery risk, helping spot gaps before a cloud incident slows restoration.

items.summary: We spoke to Darren Blanchard, the man arrested while speaking out against data centers at a community meeting. He's sharing the bodycam footage of his arrest for the first time with 404 Media.

items.title: Europe Evolves Into Ransomware's Favorite Region
items.summary: After a global lull, ransomware gangs are setting sights on a rich new arena: attacking EU organizations and their suppliers.

items.summary: Persistent cybercrime, social engineering, and infrastructure threats continue to plague the FIFA 2026 World Cup across the US, Canada, and Mexico.

items.summary: “All he has done is try to start a right-wing/conservative pressure group within Wikipedia.”

items.summary: SocGholish uses traffic distribution systems (TDSs) to provide initial access into victims' networks for cybercrime groups such as the notorious Evil Corp.

items.summary: The threat actors engineered a Golang-based sniffer to target 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and identify 110 million credentials in the ongoing global campaign.

items.summary: Threat actors can easily steal one-time passwords sent by text when they conduct a SIM swap attack. This can lead to account takeovers, so users must layer up their security measures.

items.title: Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise
items.summary: Klue's Battlecards is now the third integrated application that has been compromised to steal customers' Salesforce data, and victims include Huntress, the cybersecurity vendor.

items.title: Some text messages to look different from 1 July
items.summary: Australians will notice some text messages look different from 1 July, when new rules take effect aimed at reducing SMS impersonation scams, according to the Australian Communications and Media Authority [...]

items.title: Fileless Phantom Stealer Targets Browser Credentials
items.summary: In addition to executing entirely in memory, the malware's infection chain incorporates other anti-analysis techniques designed to evade detection.

items.summary: An open letter signed by dozens of security experts asked the government to reverse export restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.

items.summary: The emerging malware, spread via fake TikTok and Chrome downloads, has evolved by combining banking fraud with extensive device surveillance and remote control.

items.title: HTTP/2 Bomb Attacks Put Telcos, Healthcare Orgs at Risk
items.summary: The denial-of-service (DoS) exploit takes advantage of two features in HTTP/2 that were designed to save Internet bandwith, not power massive amplification attacks.

items.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
items.summary: The critical, three-stage attack is now patched, but it's part of a new group of AI prompt-injection issues that use hidden URLs and other variables.

items.title: The Beginning of the End of Social Engineering
items.summary: AI-native operating systems are shifting the responsibility to stay vigilant against social engineering cyberattacks from the user onto the system itself.
